message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.detached: Airship Helm: no Sable sub-level detected. Assemble the zeppelin first.
message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.motion: Altitude: %s m | Speed: %s m/s | Vertical: %s m/s | Heading: %s° | Pitch: %s° | Roll: %s°
message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.physics: Mass: %s | Angular velocity: X %s, Y %s, Z %s rad/s
message.zeppelin_must_have.helm.aeronaut: Aeronautics: %s balloon(s) | Capacity: %s m³ | Filled: %s m³ (%s%%) | Lift: %s
message.zeppelin_must_have.burner.status: %s | Fuel: %s (%s) | Gas output: %s m³ | Envelope range: %s blocks
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_helm_t: Reading an Airship with the Airship Helm
The Airship Helm identifies the Sable sub-level containing it
It reads position, heading, pitch, roll, linear velocity, angular velocity, and mass directly from Sable
The Helm also aggregates balloon capacity, gas fill, and lift from Create Aeronautics
Right-click with an empty hand to inspect the current flight telemetry
A Helm placed outside an assembled sub-level reports that it is detached
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_burner: Airship Burners are fuel-powered lifting-gas providers for Create Aeronautics balloons
Insert furnace fuel, Blaze Burner fuel, or Blaze Cake into the burner
Redstone enables the flame and proportionally controls gas output and fuel consumption
The burner must point into a sealed airtight envelope within its operating range
The Forced-Draft Burner uses its data-pack profile to provide greater output and operating range
The Industrial Burner is a configurable high-output tier with a correspondingly higher fuel demand
Soul-fire materials switch the burner to its blue-flame appearance
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.tag.zeppelin_s: Control, lift, navigation, propulsion, protected redstone, and service equipment for Create Aeronautics zeppelins
message.zeppelin_must_have.burner.networ: Aeronautics heat network: %s active / %s connected source(s), combined output %s m³
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_burner: Each burner remains an independent Aeronautics provider; the balloon combines every connected source in its native heater collection
Engineer's Goggles show both the selected burner reservoir and the combined output of all sources connected to the balloon
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.missi: This module has no loaded server upgrade definition
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.wrong: This upgrade is not compatible with an Airship Burner
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.slot_: The required upgrade slot is already occupied
message.zeppelin_must_have.upgrade.confl: This module conflicts with an installed upgrade
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.airship_burner: Every Airship Burner has Thermal, Airflow, and Control upgrade sockets; each socket accepts one compatible data-driven module
The Heat Recuperator trades a little peak output for lower fuel use and a larger shared heat reserve
Forced Induction raises gas output and envelope range, but increases fuel demand
The Precision Regulator reshapes the redstone throttle curve for finer low-power control
Sneak-use the Create Wrench to remove the most recently occupied upgrade socket before dismantling the burner
message.zeppelin_must_have.piped_redston: Repeater delay: %s redstone tick(s) / %s game ticks
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.piped_redstone: Right-click the repeater to cycle through one to four redstone ticks, equal to two to eight game ticks
Mixed-tier networks use the weakest segment's delay and maximum distance, so upgrading the whole trunk matters
Piped Redstone carries the complete analog signal through sealed, waterloggable conduits
Connections are explicit ports, not automatic adjacency; parallel lines can pass beside each other without merging
Use the Create Wrench on a face to open or close that exact port
Waterlogging does not wash away the conduit or interrupt its signal
Copper, Brass, and Resonant tiers progressively reduce network delay and increase repeater-free distance
A Piped Redstone Repeater preserves analog strength and starts a new distance segment after its own delay
With another conduit in hand, Create Placement Assist extends the far end of the line like a Shaft and preserves its axis
The Native Lever mounts directly to a conduit port, opens that port automatically, and powers only the attached isolated line
message.zeppelin_must_have.altitude_gaug: Altitude Gauge is not inside an assembled Sable sub-level
Altitude hold disabled; trim signal passes through unchanged
%s · altitude %s m · vertical %s m/s · trim %s → output %s
Altitude hold active: target %s m · error %s m
Altitude hold standby: target %s m · error %s m
zeppelin_must_have.goggles.altitude_gaug: Sneak + empty hand: capture or disable altitude hold
zeppelin_must_have.ponder.altitude_contr: The Altitude Gauge reads authoritative height, vertical speed, and balloon fill from the containing airship
Use the Create Wrench to cycle between altitude, vertical-speed, balloon-fill, and altitude-hold output modes
The rear face accepts a manual trim signal from a Native Lever or another analog redstone source
Sneak-right-click with an empty hand to capture the current altitude and arm the controller
The controller adds proportional altitude correction and vertical-speed damping to the trim signal
Route the front output through Piped Redstone to the airship burners for a closed-loop altitude hold
Signal slew limiting prevents abrupt burner changes and reduces oscillation
Boiler Bases fit between Create Fluid Tanks and any registered BoilerHeater
Create still calculates boiler size, water supply, engine count, efficiency, and generated Stress Units
Grade I improves a normal active heater while keeping passive heat passive
Grade II transfers more heat and reduces the number of heater columns required by large boilers
Grade III is an expensive industrial exchanger intended for maximum-level airship power plants
Boiler Bases cannot be stacked; each column requires a real heater below it
Engineer's Goggles and comparator output report the actual transferred heat
